commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Because, even because they have seduced my people, saying, Peace; and there was no peace; and one built up a wall, and, lo, others daubed it with untempered mortar: Straw. Iniquity ruins my people, (Calmet) and these do not endeavour to reform their manners. (Haydock) They ought to demolish such a work, and not daub it over. (Theodoret) One false prophet builds, and another strives to support his authority; (Junius) or God has given the people his law, but these people corrupt it. Jerome) A wall built without proper mortar, will easily be washed down: so vain hopes of security, without amendment, deceive the people. (Worthington).

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
